violations.text: Food was observed held at 43-47 degrees Fahrenheit in the refrigerator. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been in the refrigerator since the day prior. Food was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). The ambient air temperature was observed at 45 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Refrigerator was taken out of service and must not be used until it can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Refrigerator must be repaired/replaced/adjusted. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024
Chips were observed stored on the floor in the kitchen. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024

Single-service articles (food trays & soup containers) were observed stored on the floor in the kitchen. Single-service articles must be stored at least 6 inches off the ground. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024

Hot Dogs and Cheese were observed held at 43 degrees Fahrenheit in the kitchen glass door refrigerator. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods moved to colder refrigeration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2023
